text:
BOTTOM LINE — Tech rout, energy bounce, AI fears dominate tape. OpenAI revenue miss spooked chip investors; Nvidia -1-2%, Applied Materials -5.32%, Semi 7 basket -2.13% as computing cost concerns spread broadly. Oil +3.62% (USO) provided sole refuge while SPY -0.49%, QQQ -1.01%, and small caps (IWM -1.17%) all retreated—growth leadership cracking under macro scrutiny.

INDICES — VIX 17.8, down from 20.3 ma20, signals contained fear despite selloff. SPY -0.49% > DIA -0.08% > IWM -1.17% > QQQ -1.01% worst performer; large > small by 0.07pp. Most notable cross-asset move: USO +3.62% while GLD -1.86%, SLV -3.12%—risk-off rotation into commodities real assets, away from precious metals.

VOLUME — SPY vol_ratio 0.6x, QQQ 0.6x—both below 0.76 index avg, indicating weak conviction on declines. XLRE 1.0x and XLV 0.9x gaining attention; XLP 0.7x, XLF 0.6x being ignored. Vol down + price down = capitulation unfolding, not panic—distribution phase rather than washout.

SECTORS — XLE +1.66%, XLRE +0.97%, XLP +0.90%, XLV +0.26%, XLU +0.13%, XLF +0.08%, XLC -0.05%, XLY -0.70%, XLB -0.73%, XLI -0.89%, XLK -1.69%. Energy and Real Estate top two; Industrials and Tech bottom two. Oil strength carrying XLE while OpenAI fears dragging XLK lower across semiconductors and cloud names.

THE 7s — Top: Energy +1.84% (XOM, CVX leading), Old Staples +1.80% (KO +3.86% vol spike 2.3x). Bottom: Old Metals -4.24% (GLD, SLV pressured), New Metals -4.60% (lithium/rare earths). URA -4.36%, GDX -4.37% hardest hit; clear divergence in energy (oil strength vs uranium weakness).

FED & LIQUIDITY — Next FOMC today (Apr 29); CME: hold 100%, cut 0%, hike 0%. Hawkish tally 6 (Cook, Hammack, Kashkari, Logan, Barkin, Goolsbee) vs neutral 5. USD liquidity stress 63/100 elevated; CP-Tbill spread 97th percentile (0.21%) highest stress point, reserve scarcity 97th—funding markets tightening. TED 0.09%, ON-RRP $0.6B minimal.

POSITIONING — SPY PCR 1.13 (elevated protection demand), QQQ PCR 1.07, skew +5.5% and +6.7% respectively signal put premium, sustained fear premium. GEX flip SPY 648 vs spot near 708; QQQ 605 vs 780—both well above gamma triggers, dealers net long, amplifying downside into support. VVIX 91 below ma20 103.4 shows IV crush despite price decline—positioning crowded.

CROSS-ASSET — Oil +3.62% (USO) diverging sharply from gold -1.86% and silver -3.12%; typical risk-off would see both rise but commodities split across inflation vs real-asset demand lines. Dollar +0.29% (UUP) adding headwind to precious metals while supporting oil via energy import dynamics—geopolitical premium outweighing USD carry unwind.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed 62.8/100 (Greed). Top movers: SPOT -12.43% (New Staples 7, vol 6.1x), UPS -3.97% (Freight 7, vol 2.6x), KO +3.86% (Old Staples 7, vol 2.3x). 20d momentum: best XLK +23.80% sector (cracking), worst XLE -6.86% (bouncing). Fab 7 -4.23%, Semi 7 -2.13% top momentum reversals. SPOT distribution (vol spike + price down -12.43%) on subscriber guidance miss; UPS mirrors freight weakness.

WATCH — SPOT $499.39 (-12.43%): vol 6.1x, guidance miss signals consumer discretionary pain spreading; watch reversal tomorrow. AMAT $381–405.87 (-5.32%): semi selloff accelerating; gamma flip at 605 QQQ level nearby—trend break imminent if breached. UPS $x (-3.97%, vol 2.6x): freight 7 distribution pattern suggests logistics cycle rolling over; earnings call critical for guidance sustainability.
